title: Starship Flight 13: The Three Tests That Matter
product_promise: Turn a current launch announcement into three observable engineering tests using primary sources and real official footage.

beats.entry_visual: Booster 20 is already moving past the Starbase factory beneath the July 16 headline.
beats.visual_reason: Current official action proves that this is a timely Flight 13 story before any explanation begins.
beats.camera_or_motion: Official 4K rollout excerpt in a stable vertical treatment.
beats.asset_provenance: SpaceX official X post, retrieved 2026-07-15; raw and normalized hashes recorded.
beats.sound_cue: Narration and score begin; source audio is muted.
beats.transition_out: Clean cut from rollout to engine fire.
beats.fallback: Source-labelled still from the official post.

beats.entry_visual: Raptor fire is visible immediately under the reframe headline.
beats.visual_reason: The strongest spectacle becomes the visual setup for explaining why spectacle is not the success criterion.
beats.camera_or_motion: Muted official static-fire excerpt with a fixed source label.
beats.asset_provenance: SpaceX official Flight 13 announcement montage, retrieved 2026-07-15.
beats.sound_cue: Narration stays dominant; source audio is muted.
beats.transition_out: The flame clears before the FAA comparison owns the frame.

beats.entry_visual: The FAA source label, two equal cause lanes, and four-action result are visible.
beats.visual_reason: Equal lanes make the two accepted causes legible without misrepresenting a news clip as proof.
beats.camera_or_motion: Fixed-grid comparison cards enter in sequence.
beats.asset_provenance: Original Remotion layout based on the FAA statement.
beats.sound_cue: Music sits back while the causal distinction is explained.
beats.transition_out: Comparison clears before the mission checklist enters.

beats.entry_visual: Ship 40 is visible in an overhead official test shot beneath the four-fixes headline.
beats.visual_reason: A brief physical reset keeps the corrective-action sentence connected to the real vehicle.
beats.camera_or_motion: Muted official excerpt with a fixed Ship 40 source label.
beats.asset_provenance: SpaceX official Flight 13 announcement montage, range 16.0–19.0 seconds.
beats.sound_cue: Narration remains dominant; source audio is muted.
beats.transition_out: Clean cut into the three mission objectives.

beats.entry_visual: The full three-step scaffold is visible with the first objective active.
beats.visual_reason: The viewer leaves knowing the exact three operations to watch during the mission.
beats.camera_or_motion: Three stable rows activate in sequence.
beats.asset_provenance: Original Remotion checklist using the official SpaceX mission plan.
beats.sound_cue: Each objective lands on one narration phrase.
beats.transition_out: Clean cut from checklist to a brief archived launch reset.

beats.entry_visual: An explicitly labelled archived Starship test ignition is visible.
beats.visual_reason: A final real-world reset keeps the conclusion attached to the physical vehicle.
beats.camera_or_motion: Muted official archive excerpt in the same vertical treatment.
beats.asset_provenance: SpaceX official montage, excerpt labelled archive to prevent temporal confusion.
beats.sound_cue: Music lifts slightly; source remains muted.
beats.transition_out: Archive clears into the light conclusion.

beats.entry_visual: The full repeatability payoff and Aaron identity are visible on the ivory field.
beats.visual_reason: The end converts launch spectacle into one durable operating insight.
beats.camera_or_motion: Restrained line reveal and quiet hold.
beats.asset_provenance: Original Aaron editorial end card based on the verified NASA HLS relationship.
